message:
refactor(h2/hpack): hoist dynamic table size update parsing into decode() preamble

RFC 7541 §4.2 specifies that dynamic table size updates are only valid at
the beginning of a header block, before any header field representations.
The previous implementation handled this inside decode_header() using a
loop with an allow_size_updates flag, which had two problems:

1. A header block containing only size updates (no headers) was rejected
   with "size update without header", but RFC 7541 permits update-only
   blocks — they simply resize the table and produce zero headers.

2. decode_header() was an iterative loop that mixed size-update parsing
   with header decoding, making the control flow harder to follow and
   the allow_size_updates threading fragile.

This refactor:
- Moves the size-update consumption loop into decode() as a preamble that
  runs before any header decoding. Updates are consumed greedily while the
  0x20 prefix pattern is detected.
- Simplifies decode_header() from a loop to a single-dispatch function
  that handles only indexed, literal-with-indexing, literal-never-indexed,
  and literal-without-indexing representations.
- If a 0x20 byte is encountered inside decode_header() (after headers have
  started), it correctly returns a COMPRESSION_ERROR per §4.2.
- Retains the MAX_SIZE_UPDATES (16) DoS guard as a module-level constant.

Test changes:
- "size_update_without_header" test renamed and now asserts success with
  empty headers and the expected table size
- New test: multiple consecutive size updates without headers correctly
  applies the last value (512) to the dynamic table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
